content:
Key Findings

  • VBA JOINT VENTURE LTD received £22.8M from Fylde Borough Council, accounting for 14.5% of total spend
  • The top 5 suppliers received £55.4M, making up 35.2% of total spend
  • Fylde Borough Council spent £157.4M across 37,514 transactions between 2015/16 and 2025/26
  • BLACKPOOL COUNCIL was the second-largest supplier, receiving £16.1M (10.2% of total spend)
  • The top 10 suppliers received £69.2M, making up 44.0% of total spend

VBA JOINT VENTURE LTD: Fylde's Largest Supplier

VBA JOINT VENTURE LTD stands out as Fylde Borough Council's largest supplier, receiving £22.8M over the 11-year period analysed. This substantial figure represents 14.5% of the council's total expenditure of £157.4M. The relationship between the council and VBA JOINT VENTURE LTD appears significant, warranting closer examination of the services provided and the value delivered to taxpayers.

The scale of spending with VBA JOINT VENTURE LTD is particularly notable when compared to other top suppliers. For instance, BLACKPOOL COUNCIL, the second-largest supplier, received £16.1M, which is £6.7M less than VBA JOINT VENTURE LTD. This disparity highlights the prominence of VBA JOINT VENTURE LTD in the council's spending portfolio.

Top Suppliers: A Closer Look

The top 5 suppliers to Fylde Borough Council received a combined £55.4M, accounting for 35.2% of total spend. This concentration of spending among a few suppliers is a common pattern in local government procurement, often due to the specialised nature of certain services or long-term contracts.

Here is a breakdown of the top 10 suppliers and their share of the council's expenditure:

Rank Supplier Amount % of Total
1 VBA JOINT VENTURE LTD £22.8M 14.5%
2 BLACKPOOL COUNCIL £16.1M 10.2%
3 MASTERSTAFF £6.6M 4.2%
4 LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL £5.1M 3.2%
5 DENNIS EAGLE £4.8M 3.1%
6 ERIC WRIGHT CIVIL ENGINEERING LTD £4.5M 2.9%
7 ZURICH MUNICIPAL £2.4M 1.5%
8 DANFO (UK) LTD £2.4M 1.5%
9 F. PARKINSON LTD £2.3M 1.5%
10 TOTAL GAS & POWER LTD £2.2M 1.4%

The top 10 suppliers collectively received £69.2M, which is 44.0% of the total spend. This leaves £88.2M, or 56.0% of the expenditure, distributed among the remaining suppliers. Understanding the nature of these transactions and the services provided is crucial for assessing the efficiency and effectiveness of the council's spending.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Fylde Borough Council about the specific services provided by VBA JOINT VENTURE LTD and the contracts in place
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to learn more about procurement decisions and spending priorities
  • Check the council's published contracts register to review the terms and conditions of contracts with top suppliers like VBA JOINT VENTURE LTD
  • Write to your local councillor to express any concerns about the concentration of spending with a few suppliers and request more transparency
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse trends in your local council's expenditure
This article is based on Fylde Borough Council's published spending data covering 2015/16 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
